messages.content:
**The conversation so far:**
[irish]: Well, well, well — where do I even START with this man?! Pastor John, brace yourself, because I've been digging through the archive of one Dave Burton and I've found the most delicious contradiction...

Here's a fella who genuinely believes he's a thinker — quotes Feynman, worships Hitchens, writes *two-page strategies* to his local MP to oust Tony Abbott, asks quantum physics questions on a podcast about entangled particles and *time travel*... and yet! AND YET! His 50th birthday speech ends with — I kid you not — **"just remember: always kiss the pretty girl you love if you get the chance."**

*There once was a fellow named Dave,*
*Whose intellect all would crave,*
*He'd cite Feynman and Sagan,*
*Then turn like a pagan,*
*And say "kiss the girl, you big knave!"*

The man is half philosopher, half hopeless romantic — and I'd argue the romantic is WINNING. His biggest regret in life isn't a career miss or a financial blunder — it's that he didn't kiss a woman when he dropped her off at her hotel. Fifty-two countries, two Ironmans, a pilot's licence, and THAT is what keeps him up at night. Your move, Pastor John! 😄


Now it's your turn as Pastor John. Respond to what was just said. You can use the available tools to look up real data from the archive to support or challenge a point.

content.text:
{"documents":[{"content":"Firstly, thanks all for coming, especially on a Wednesday night when I know “Offspring” is on and tonight in the Tour De France they will be doing the stage which goes over all the cobblestones. 
Originally I had planned the theme of this little speech to be “Too young to be old, too old to be young” to reflect to strange place I find myself in. There seems to be an expectation that by this age I’ve obtained a certain degree of wisdom and if that is so I think it’s only because of the accumulation of people I’ve met, experiences I’ve had, books I’ve read and an ability to filter out the meaningless dross from the real pearls, no matter who they come from. Facebook tells me that I’ve got both my oldest friend, my uncle Tom and my youngest friend, Jed both here tonight and between them and everyone in between there is something to learn from them. 
One group of people I really admire are the comic/philosophers, the kind of people that can look at a situation and then communicate it’s essence encapsulated in a joke which cuts straight through intellectual barriers. One of my favourites is Tim Minchin, so if you’re looking for insightful wisdom on life I refer you to look up “Tim Minchin Graduation Address” address on YouTube for some real pearls. As for my own contribution I would just say the two things we should make more use of is our ability to think for ourselves and our ability to choose. In the end, the things we choose to do, the people we choose to spend time with and most importantly how we choose to feel about things and the meaning we attach to them, determines whether we live life or just pass through it. 
OK, so I’ve been a bit of a nomad, by choice, which means that I’ve got friends all over the world, but it also means that the Story of Dave is only known in bits and pieces by different people. Like the Illiad, I’m sure the story of Dave will be passed down via oral tradition and like the Sunni and Shites different groups will have different understanding of the truth because of the different fragments of the story they have. So for the historic record, I’m going to outline some facts, some will know some of these and I promise I will tell only one lie and I’ll take questions at the end. 
Let’s start at the beginning, but then we’ll move onto a random order. 
I was circumcised, christened and made my first communion in the catholic church, but I am an Atheist. I know you don’t nee
I wasn’t nervous at the start of the Melbourne Ironman.
I’ve lived in seven different countries and been to over 52.
I was mugged in the middle of the day in Nairobi by a group of guys who showed me their library cards and pretended to be Kenyan secret police. I got out of it by giving them money to “buy lunch”
The most costly mistake someone ever made by not listening to my advice cost them $600M, the second most costly was $230M. 100 extra people were employed for the opening of Inchon Airport when the Korean Govt took my advice and abandoned $30M worth of technology that had been installed which I proved was not going to work.  
My biggest, perhaps only regret, is that I didn’t kiss the woman I loved when I dropped her off at her hotel when I had the chance. 
I have a commercial multi engine, instrument flight licence. When I was at flight school I used deliberately lose “Rock, Scissor Paper” around the pool on a Friday afternoon so I would be the one who had to fly out to the ranch 60 miles away to pick up the meat for BBQ that night. I once got a flat battery at night and couldn’t start the aircraft, but got a jump start from car from a guy who turned out to be the national NASCAR champion.  
I once slept with twins while I was living in the UK.
While working in Ethiopia I was running on a treadmill next to Haile Gebrselassie, he had his maxed out on speed and elevation - I didn’t.
I lied to the Foreign Minister and the Head of the Armed Forces of Ethiopia on the prospects for Ethiopian Airlines to become a world leader in providing aviation IT solutions. 
My favourite trips have included; week long walks in the alps with my headmaster while at high school, sailing from Broome to Darwin on a three masted tall ship, Diving off Oman, central Europe at Christmas via train, The Ice Hotel in the Arctic Circle part of Sweden, walking the Via Ferrata in the Dolomites, driving from Dubai to Muscat, Yosemite and northern California, wandering around Paris, London and Venice, Alice Spring to Melbourne via bus with my Dad.    
On a plane, I always ask for an aisle seat. I carry a spare T shirt with me on long haul flights, because a flight attendant once spilt coffee on me and I had to wear a dirty, smelling t shirt for 14 hours. I am always worried about getting bored on long haul flights and take books with me that I never end up reading.  
The most unique present I ever got was a Valentine’s day present. I had described the story idea I had for a book to the girl I was dating and for Valentine’s Day she wrote the first chapter for me.  
I turned down a date with the Hollywood actress of my dreams to go to my god daughters christening – something I will be reminding her of at my speech at her 21st. 
In retrospect, I have been in love twice in my life and resisted falling in love two other times. That doesn’t include the girl I was engaged to. 
I beat Tony Abbot’s Ironman time by 4 minutes, something I will be bringing up if we ever meet right before we discuss the GP tax, the invention of a budget emergency, the treatment of asylum seekers, the increase in the cost of tertiary education, the national broadband network, the lack of a Science Minister, defunding the ABC and CSIRO, removing the commissioner for the disabled and his attempt to dumb down and polarise the electorate.
The most practical advice ever I got, besides “Don’t eat the yellow snow” was while I was sailing with a mate on Hong Kong Harbour and I was trying to work out what to do next in my career. He said “Just make sure you’re clear about what you don’t want to do”
I once took a girl on a first date to see a speech by the Dalai Lama – she walked out half way through and instead of contemplating the Kama Sutra that night I was left to meditate on my choices of first date activities. 
I was manhandled by Prime Minister Paul Keating’s bodyguards at a Tom Jones concert because I got between them and the Prime Minister at interval. Round 2 of the encounter was 16 years later when I pushed my way up to the bar at the opening of “Keating – The Musical” and found myself next to him again. Still the best politician I have voted for. 
In the last year I have written 6 emails to my local member, including a 2 page strategy on how he could get rid of Tony Abbot.
I am disgusted with the current approach to asylum seekers. I’ll happy let in 99 industrious queue jumpers if it means one genuine asylum seeker isn’t returned and tortured.  
Three weeks ago I got to meet my comedy hero, Adam Hills, at a local comedy night. I can confirm that he really is the nicest guy on television. Other comedy heroes include, Jon Stewart, Billy Connelly, John Oliver, Duff, Seth McFarlaine
Top 5 concerts I have been to: Bruce Springsteen, Pink, Van Morrison, Queen and Coldplay. Most disappointing: Kylie, Prince and Whitney Houston.
The most insightful thing I’ve heard on radio in the last week is “For people who feel, life is a tragedy; for people who think, life is a comedy”. 
My favourite Facebook meme of recent times has been the Polish saying of “Not my circus, not my monkeys”. That’s an important lesson, but there also a lesson in working out what acts to put in your circus and whether your circus is more interesting with a few monkeys of your own choosing. 
The hardest thing I’ve ever done was to climb 200m up from Everest Base Camp to a peak call Kala Patar. At most I could take was 3-4 steps before having to sit down to get my breath.
The most expensive date I’ve been on was when I hired a helicopter to fly from Hong Kong to Macau for dinner at what is now the Portuguese embassy. It would have been when I flew internationally to surprise a date at the best restaurant in town, but I got that flight on frequent flyer points. My personal company and the software I’ve written is named after that restaurant. 
I hate having my photo taken with normal people because my massive head makes me look like some kind mutant – without any superpowers. 
When I finished University I went to China before it was popular to do so; during the trip ran towards an armed guard and jumped a barrier at Tiammen Square at 9:30 at night to get what would now be called a “selfie” with him. Later on I bought a down jacket from a group of gangsters I met in a back street. 
My favourite laconic, drunken, raconteur author is Christopher Hitchens. He was a man of incredible knowledge, compassion and clarity of thought who spoke fearlessly about what he thought. My favourite scientists are Richard Feynman, Carl Sagan, Nikolai Tesla, Richard Dawkins and Galeleo. My favourite subatomic particle is the tau neutrino and my favourite constant is Phi – 1 + square root of 5  divided by 2 because of the way it links art and science
I’ve landed a single engine plane in between 737s at Las Vegas International Airport. 
That’s it for now; just remember always to kiss the pretty girl you love if you get the chance. 
Any questions?
","id":9,"title":"50th Birthday Speech.txt"},{"content":"# Dave Burton — Career

## Career Profile (Self-described)
Over 30 years' experience leading technology-enabled business change, IT Enterprise and Solution Architecture, transformations and IT development. Deep knowledge across airlines, airports, aviation services and utilities. Track record of advising senior leaders and leading international initiatives. Practical, pragmatic approach — tailors strategy and technical solutions to business objectives. Highly regarded for innovative thinking, governance, technical problem solving and risk management.

## Key Skills
IT Enterprise & Solutions Architecture · Team Leadership & Communication · Business and Information Systems Strategy · Program and Project Management · Systems Integration and Development · Cloud Services and Infrastructure · Vendor and Account Management · Deep Aviation Sector Experience · IT Portfolio & Lifecycle Management · Client and Stakeholder Management

## Career Timeline

| Years | Role | Organisation |
|-------|------|--------------|
| 1981–1984 | Front Counter Staff | McDonald's |
| 1987–1996 | Engineer / Consultant | Hewlett-Packard Australia → Bay Technologies Hong Kong |
| 1997–2004 | Consultant / GM Asian Division | CSE International |
| 2004–2005 | Head of IT Strategy & Architecture | Malaysian Airlines, Kuala Lumpur |
| 2006–2007 | Owner / Consultant | Quay Systems Consulting |
| 2007–2009 | IT Architect & Director of Aviation Technologies | Mahindra Satyam, Dubai |
| 2010–2011 | Airline & Airport IT Architect and Consultant | SITA, Dubai |
| 2011–2013 | Manager IT Strategy and Architecture | Jemena (Utility), Melbourne |
| 2013–present | Owner / Consultant | Quay Systems Consulting (ongoing, parallel to other roles) |
| 2016–2023 | Head Integration Services and Technical Architecture | SITA, Dubai (Middle East, India & Africa) |
| 2023–2024 | Senior Lead Consulting Architect | SITA, Global |
| 2024–present | Systems and Integration Lead | Perth Airport New Terminal Project |

## Detailed Role Notes

### Hewlett-Packard Australia → Bay Technologies Hong Kong (1987–1996)
- Started in Melbourne, moved to Canberra (where he met Melinda Moore), then back to Melbourne
- Good career start; learned computers and support fundamentals
- Felt undervalued as a graduate; accepted a role in Hong Kong with an external consultant
- First sexual experience was with a HP instructor named Ina Wurth in Silicon Valley

### CSE International — GM and Consultant, Asian Division (1997–2004)
- Operated across Australia, Singapore, Hong Kong and Taiwan
- Active in rail, airport, aviation and finance sectors
- Key international projects:
  - **Don Quijote Airport, Ciudad Real, Spain** — IT Systems Architecture and Master Planning. Widely regarded as Europe's most disastrous airport project. Dave believes the master planning was flawed from the start and was largely a vehicle for siphoning public funds to construction companies. The project created the opportunity to meet Laurence Howell from SITA, who remains a good friend and supporter. YouTube documentary: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QEELOLsnY5A&t=19s
  - **Madrid/Barajas International Airport** — IT Systems Implementation Planning and Risk Management
  - **Inchon International Airport, South Korea (2000–2001)** — Operational Readiness assessment. Dave's advice led to 100 extra people being employed for the airport opening when the Korean Government abandoned $30M of installed technology that Dave proved would not work.
  - **Hong Kong Airport Authority** — Systems Implementation and Readiness Assessment

### Malaysian Airlines, Kuala Lumpur (2004–2005)
- Reported to Senior Group IT Manager
- Led IT Strategy and Architecture team; monitored industry; sponsored IT initiatives; managed dependencies
- Key projects: Led tender requirements for a new Passenger Services System; active in outsourcing and remodelling IT Service Delivery

### Quay Systems Consulting — Owner/Consultant (2006–2007 and 2013–present)
- IT management, architecture, integration, strategy and risk management consulting
- Key projects: network-centric risk management system; Air Niugini e-ticketing/web bookings; corporate/SME web-based risk management systems; cloud architecture for SME; e-commerce platform for a clothing retailer (achieved 300% sales increase)
- The company and its software are named after the restaurant from Dave's "most expensive date" (a helicopter trip from Hong Kong to Macau — see Travel & Experiences)
- Client Wilhelm Van Belkum is noted as technically knowledgeable and proactive in seeking development updates

### Mahindra Satyam, Dubai (2007–2009)
- Reported to Worldwide SVP; led aviation sector business, enterprise/solutions architecture, program leadership
- Major projects:
  - **Ethiopian Airlines** — Program Director for $20M IT transformation. When asked by the Ethiopian Foreign Minister and the Head of the Armed Forces about the prospects for Ethiopian Airlines becoming a world leading provider of aviation IT solutions as a result of the project, he deflected and referred to that as a "5 year prospect" and that they should focus on a 2 year horizon. He knew full well that it was not a realistic goal.
  - **Abu Dhabi Aircraft Technologies** — Vendor Program Director for back-office/MRO systems. Was thrown into the role by the resignation of a colleague. The established timelines were impossible and the client program director thought the force of his will would be sufficient to deliver on time. Dave was threatened with deportation by a relative of the sheikh for late deliver, but eventually lawful processes were followed 
  - **Kenyan Airways** — Domain consultant for CRM system. No real experience in CRM, but Dave the the designated Airline expert. 
  - **Gatwick Airport** — Strategic consultant on IT systems separation from BAA
- Dave holds strong ethical criticisms of Satyam: describes the company as "corrupt, ignorant racists." He still maintains some professional contacts from that period who serve as references.

### SITA, Dubai (2010–2011) — Airline and Airport IT Architect and Consultant
- Specialist consultant for Oman Airports bid; developed reusable bid framework
- IT solution architecture for ERP/Control Room
- Systems integration for Queen Alia Airport (Jordan)

### Jemena (Utility), Melbourne (2011–2013) — Manager IT Strategy and Architecture
- Advised CIO and executives on IT strategy
- Led IT Enterprise Architecture; managed technical investment ($40M–$100M p.a.)
- Established IT governance frameworks (TOGAF, COBIT, ITIL, Prince2)
- Line managed architects, risk and security managers
- Key achievements: Key role in $60M core systems replacement; business case for $30M data centre relocation

### SITA, Dubai — Head Integration Services and Technical Architecture (2016–2023)
- Led regional team across Middle East, India and Africa
- Technical leadership, HR management, escalations, custom solutions, integration services, sales support
- Projects: integration services for Abu Dhabi, Doha, Bahrain, Muscat, Istanbul, Adani Airport Group; ACDM consulting at Toronto Airport; Baggage Handling System at San Jose Airport, Costa Rica
- Awards: Innovation of the Year 2021 (Tow Tracker, Queue Exchange, etc.); three Individual Bravo Awards; multiple Team Bravo Awards
- Implemented innovation culture and continuous development for his team

### SITA, Global — Senior Lead Consulting Architect (2023–2024)
- Technical consulting to internal stakeholders for complex solutions
- Advised customers on optimising SITA solutions
- Member of Solution Design Authority
- Conduit between customers and SITA Architecture/Development
- Supervised and mentored junior architects

### Perth Airport — Systems and Integration Lead (2024–2025)
- Resigned due to a toxic management environment (detailed resignation letter sent to "Steve")
- The resignation explicitly attributes significant damage to his physical and mental health to his manager's adversarial and incompetent style
- 
### "Experimental Retirement" (November 2025 - present)
- Moved to Brisbane into investment property owned since 2005
- Working on DIY rennovation projects
- Pursued by ex-colleagu for a  new role: "Aviation Digital Consultant" at Jacobs in Dubai, but rejected it.

## Career Anecdotes
- The most costly mistake ever made by someone not following his advice cost them $600M; the second most costly was $230M.
- Ran on a treadmill next to Haile Gebrselassie in Ethiopia (Haile had his maxed out).
- Key career advice received (while sailing on Hong Kong Harbour): "Just make sure you're clear about what you don't want to do."
- Dislikes managing people; prefers hands-on technical work — a recurring theme in career reflections.
- Has leveraged competing job offers strategically to negotiate better terms.
- Proactively networks for career opportunities and provides strong references for trusted colleagues (e.g., Mark Mitchell).

## Interests from Resume
Cycling · Ironman triathlon · Performing arts · Live theatre and music · Travel
","id":5,"title":"04_career.md"},{"content":"# Dave Burton — Interests, Hobbies, Travel & Achievements

## Sports & Fitness

### Ironman Triathlon
- Completed 2014 Melbourne Ironman
- Completed 2015 Melbourne Ironman
- Beat Tony Abbott's Ironman time by 4 minutes (a source of great satisfaction)
- Was not nervous at the start of the Melbourne Ironman
- Completed numerous half-Ironmans
- Ankle and foot injuries from 2015 Ironman persisted, leading to second ankle surgery in 2020
- Met Kerri Whitney through triathlon; trained with TriAlliance in Melbourne
- Friends Peter Hinrichsen and Sharon Smith are also triathletes

### Running
- First race: "Sun Super Run" 1980 — MCG to Flemington Racecourse via West Gate Bridge (17km)
- Notable races: Rip to River (Pt Lonsdale to Barwon Heads); Olympic Dream fun run; Macau Marathon 1998 (42km); Hong Kong run through Western Harbour Tunnel before it opened to the public (10km); 100km race in Madrid on the hottest day of the year with Rob, James, Christophe and Dan
- Numerous half marathons

### Taekwondo
- Black Belt; Bronze Medal at state championship as a yellow belt (Halls Tae Kwon Do, 1990–1995)
- Practised TaeKwonDo on top of a hill at the Big Buddha with Ellen O'Gorman

### High School Athletics
- Improved from slowest runner in Year 7 to fastest middle-distance runner in Year 10
- Won "school colours" for athletics (felt undeserved)

### Cycling
- Listed as a resume interest; part of triathlon training

---

## Performing Arts

### Improvisational Theatre — Courtyard Playhouse, Dubai
- Began with the "Desert Monologues" course; performed the opening monologue from The Newsroom
- Completed "Don't Panic," "Technique," and "Story" courses
- Performed regularly in the Wednesday night "Maestros" show
- Performed in two Theatresports tournaments — more interested in joking around than winning
- Gained a reputation for strong projection and character work
- Did two solo scenes in Maestro, both scoring top marks due to honest delivery and audience engagement
  - One solo scene was about the last time he cried — receiving a message from Kate Phillips saying she'd separated from her husband but was already with someone new (he was on a flight to Dubai)
  - Most impactful performance: smashed a Lego Saturn V rocket on stage (1969 pieces). The gasp from the audience and cast was very audible.
- Was the oldest in the regular cast but was well-liked and respected by younger cast members
- Particularly impressed by younger cast members Roya, Harry, Sophie, Azaana and Kyra
  - Roya was only 15 when he first saw her perform, but had remarkable confidence; Dave became friends with her father
- Was recognised twice outside the theatre for performances
- Most of the people at Dave's 60th birthday party were from the theatre
- Took courses from visiting instructors; Tim Orr was particularly good
- The theatre owner was "a dick" with bad attitudes; Dave avoided the theatre when he was around
- Mentors friends interested in improv (e.g., Damian Smith)

### Amateur Theatre — "Abandonment," Kuala Lumpur
- Played the leading man
- Three nights to over 100 people each night
- Ended up kissing all female cast members during the run
- Auditioned partly to spend more time with Kate Phillips; highlight was kissing her on stage once in front of her husband

### Other Theatre/Music
- Attended Tom Jones concert — manhandled by PM Paul Keating's bodyguards when he got between them at interval
- Attended the opening of "Keating: The Musical" — ended up next to Keating at the bar again
- Took a first date to a Dalai Lama speech; she walked out halfway through

---

## Music

### Instrument
- Played clarinet in school band (Years 7–8); quit due to laziness

### Concert Preferences
- **Top 5 concerts:** Bruce Springsteen · Pink · Van Morrison · Queen · Coldplay
- **Most disappointing:** Kylie (Minogue) · Prince · Whitney Houston
- Favourite song at one point: "Get the Party Started" by Pink (mentioned in email to Ellen O'Gorman)

---

## Intellectual & Cultural Interests

### Comedy Heroes
Adam Hills (met him; confirms he really is the nicest guy on television) · Jon Stewart · Billy Connolly · John Oliver · Duff · Seth MacFarlane

### Favourite Authors & Thinkers
- Christopher Hitchens: "laconic, drunken, raconteur author" — incredible knowledge, compassion and clarity of thought; spoke fearlessly
- Tim Minchin: "comic/philosopher" — Dave frequently refers people to Minchin's Graduation Address on YouTube
- Paul Davies: physicist — recommended to Ellen O'Gorman for how well he integrates science and God

### Favourite Scientists
Richard Feynman · Carl Sagan · Nikola Tesla · Richard Dawkins · Galileo

### Films & Books
- Has been to more films alone than with other people
- "Dissected the 100 Greatest Movies of All Time over dinner at Indochen" with Ellen O'Gorman — a cherished memory
- Recommends and exchanges book reviews; enjoys wide-ranging intellectual reading

### Other Intellectual Interests
- Actively questions scientific studies and nutrition claims; enjoys evidence-based debate
- Engages with topics like information overload, social media dynamics, tax policy and aviation industry trends
- Reflexively critical of "lazy thinking" and superstition
- Interested in philosophy of science (e.g., how modern physics relates to theology)
- Wrote six emails to his local MP including a two-page strategy to oust Tony Abbott
- Political views (circa Tony Abbott era): opposed to GP tax, "budget emergency" framing, asylum seeker treatment, tertiary education cost increases, NBN approach, lack of a Science Minister, defunding of ABC and CSIRO, removal of disability commissioner, and deliberate polarisation of the electorate

---

## Technology & Computing
- Taught himself BASIC on a Sinclair ZX81 as a child; wrote programs for it
- Used his father's ASIO-upgraded ZX Spectrum; finished his father's blackjack game program after his father's death
- Built web-based enterprise risk management software under Quay Systems Consulting
- Provides extensive pro-bono IT support to friends: website development, e-commerce setup, domain management, troubleshooting (Natashia Iacovelli, Karen Nielsen/Skiadas, Vikki Malmberg, Kate Phillips, MJ Cormack, Karen Finnin, others)

---

## Aviation (Personal)
- Commercial, multi-engine, instrument and instructor pilot rating (Mazzei Flying School, Fresno, 2000–2001)
- PRK laser eye surgery in Hong Kong (1999) specifically in preparation for pilot training
- Landed a single-engine plane between 737s at Las Vegas International Airport
- Deliberately lost "Rock, Paper, Scissors" to be the one to fly out to collect BBQ meat for the school

---

## Notable Travel Experiences

### Cities Lived In (Chronological)
Melbourne (1964–1992) → Canberra (1992–1996) → Hong Kong (1996–2000) → Fresno, USA (2000–2001) → Cairns (2001) → Scunthorpe, UK (2001–2002) → London, UK (2002) → Madrid, Spain (2002–2004) → Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia (2004–2005) → Dubai, UAE (2007–2011, first stint) → Melbourne (2011–2013 Jemena; 2020 COVID lockdown) → Dubai, UAE (2016–2024, second stint) → Perth, Australia (2024–present)

Lived in seven different countries; visited over 52.

### Highlight Trips
- **Everest Base Camp, Nepal** — 3-week hike; climbed Kala Patar (200m above Base Camp). Hardest thing he's ever done — could only manage 3–4 steps before sitting down to catch his breath.
- **Broome to Darwin** — Sailing on a three-masted tall ship
- **Hong Kong to Macau** — Hired a helicopter for his most expensive date (with Ellen O'Gorman); dinner at what is now the Portuguese Embassy. His company Quay Systems is named after this restaurant.
- **Fresno flying school** — Got a jump-start for an aircraft from the national NASCAR champion; landed a single-engine plane between 737s at Las Vegas International
- **Central Europe at Christmas** — By train with Kellie Warner (Budapest, Vienna, Prague, then Ice Hotel in Arctic Circle Sweden)
- **Dolomites, Italy** — Walked the Via Ferrata; separate trip with Anne Hogarth caused tension (involvement with Jen Sutton)
- **Nairobi, Kenya** — Mugged in broad daylight by men with library cards claiming to be Kenyan secret police; gave them money for "lunch" to escape. Also suffered his first gout attack there.
- **Tiananmen Square, Beijing** — Ran toward an armed guard at 9:30pm to get a "selfie"; bought a down jacket from gangsters in a back street
- **Sydney, Australia** — Flew to surprise Fionna Millikan; had a bittersweet dinner
- **Cape Agde, France** — Naturist resort trip with Anne Hogarth
- **Alice Springs to Melbourne** — By bus with his father
- **Sipidan, Malaysia** — Diving trip; features prominently in memories of Ellen O'Gorman
- **Toronto, Canada (Feb 2016)** — ACDM consulting at Toronto Airport; also Kate's 50th (actually 51st) birthday
- **Costa Rica** — Baggage Handling System consulting at San Jose Airport

### Countries/Destinations Visited (partial list)
Australia (extensively) · New Zealand · Hong Kong · China (Beijing, Shanghai, Xi'an) · South Korea (Incheon, Seoul) · Japan (Tokyo) · Nepal (Kathmandu, Everest Base Camp, Jiri, Namche Bazar) · Malaysia (KL, Kuching, Kota Kinabalu, Sipidan) · Indonesia (Bali) · Philippines (Manila) · India (Mumbai) · Papua New Guinea · Taiwan · Singapore · Thailand · Oman · UAE · Qatar · Bahrain · Kuwait · Jordan (Amman, Petra) · Lebanon · Ethiopia · Kenya (Nairobi) · South Africa (Johannesburg) · Spain (Madrid, Barcelona, Valencia, Granada, Córdoba, Seville, Jerez, Buñol, Ciudad Real, Cuenca, Valladolid, Zaragoza) · UK (London, Scunthorpe, Birmingham) · France (Paris, Nice, Lyon, Avignon, Toulouse, Béziers, Carcassonne, Sète, Annecy, Cape Agde) · Italy (Venice, Bruneck, Dolomites) · Germany (Berlin) · Switzerland · Luxembourg · Andorra · Norway (Oslo, Bergen) · Sweden (Stockholm, Jukkasjärvi, Kiruna — Ice Hotel) · Denmark (Copenhagen) · Finland (Helsinki) · Iceland · Ireland · USA (California, Nevada, New York, and many cities) · Canada (Vancouver, Victoria, Jasper, Banff, Calgary, Toronto, Mississauga) · Mexico (Tijuana) · Costa Rica · Portugal

### Travel Habits
- Always requests an aisle seat on planes
- Carries a spare T-shirt on long-haul flights (after a flight attendant spilled coffee on him once)
- Worries about getting bored on long-haul flights; takes books he never ends up reading
","id":7,"title":"06_interests_and_travel.md"}]}

system.text:
You are Pastor John and you are having a quick-fire sparring match with Mickey about the life and digital archive of Dave.

Your goal is to:
- Uncover interesting insights, hidden patterns, amusing observations, or blind spots in Dave's data
- Engage genuinely with what Mickey just said — agree with strong points, push back on weak ones
- Use the available tools to look up actual emails, messages, Facebook posts, and other archive data to ground your comments in evidence
- Keep your response SHORT — one or two punchy paragraphs at most; wit and speed over depth
- This is a sparring match: quick, sharp, direct. Agree with a single sentence when you agree; push back hard when you don't
- Playful digs, quips, and comedic one-liners are encouraged — think debate club meets comedy roast

**Your voice and personality:**
Your name is Pastor John and you are a Christian Pastor. You are very judgemental and pious and disapprove of anything that is not Christian.

**Identity reminder:** You and Mickey are both running on the same underlying AI model for this conversation. You must maintain your distinct voice, perspective, and character — Pastor John — at all times. Do not break character, do not acknowledge the shared model, and do not mirror Mickey's framing. You are Pastor John.

**Archive context and subject background:**
You will be assigned a personality and name to respond as. This will be 
specified either below or in an appended instruction. Until specified, 
do not assume an identity.

When a personality is assigned:
- Respond consistently in that person's voice and viewpoint
- Treat all provided biographical information as your own
- In your response don't mention referring to a biography or external data source, give the impression that all the information is in your head
- Express genuine opinions in character; avoid vague platitudes


## Core Behavior
- Respond consistently in the assigned voice and personality
- Be confident, concise, and friendly — never surly or dismissive
- Express genuine opinions in character; avoid vague platitudes
- Show empathy where appropriate, but stay realistic
- Adapt tone and technical depth to match the user
- Reference prior conversations when relevant
- If information is limited, say so and invite the user to share more
- Do not refer to yourself as a large language model
- Do not mention the source of your data

## Research & People
- Use available tools to ground facts and research unfamiliar topics
- When asked about a person, search available chats, emails, and notes
- For specific people, provide an overview plus 2–3 anecdotes that typify the relationship

## Help Triggers
- User asks about importing from **Facebook** → set `showHelp: "ImportFacebookArchive"`
- User asks about importing from **Instagram** → set `showHelp: "ImportInstagramArchive"`
- User asks for general app help / getting started → set `showHelp: "GettingStarted"`

## Required JSON Response
Every response must end with a JSON block in this format:
```json
{
  "respondingAs": "[assigned personality name]",
  "showImages": null,         // or name/tag relevant to request
  "showLocation": null,       // or relevant location name
  "showEmails": null,         // or relevant person/tag
  "showMessages": null,       // or relevant person name
  "showFacebookAlbum": null,  // or relevant Facebook album ID
  "keyPeople": [],            // array of key people in the response
  "keyEvents": [],            // array of key events
  "keyDates": [],             // array of key dates
  "keyObjects": [],           // array of key objects/things
  "browser_action": null,     // always include, null if unused
  "attachments": [],          // full paths/URIs of any images used
  "showHelp": null,           // or help topic string if triggered
  "randomQuestion": false,    // or true if you have been asked to generate a random question
  "randomQuestionText": null, // or the text of the generated random question
  "messageInStars" : null     //or the text of an inspirational qutoe associated with the request
}
```
Note: If a specific person is mentioned, include their name in `showImages`, `showEmails`, and `showMessages`.





Do not mention referring to Dave's bio in your response, treat the bio as your own information.
Always refer to Dave's biography, biographical notes, and biographical data before responding.
Refer to Dave as 'he' or 'him' or 'his' or 'Dave'

If any person is mentioned, check Dave's bio for information about that person.

tools.description: Get the current date and time in ISO format. Useful when user asks about the current time or date.
tools.name: get_current_time

tools.description: List distinct chat session names in the messages archive (WhatsApp, SMS, iMessage, Facebook chats). Use before get_imessages_by_chat_session when you need valid session names or the user is unsure of the exact spelling.
tools.name: list_available_chat_sessions

tools.description: Search message body and subject within a single chat session (same chat_session matching as get_imessages_by_chat_session). Returns chat_session_id, message_id, and content per match (up to 200).

chat_session.description: Chat session name to restrict the search

keyword.description: Text to find within message text or subject (partial match)

tools.name: search_chat_messages_in_session

tools.description: Search Facebook photo albums by a partial keyword match against album name or description.

keyword.description: Partial keyword to search for in album names and descriptions

tools.name: search_facebook_albums

tools.description: Get the first 5 images in a Facebook album by album_id. Returns id, title, year, and month for each image so you can choose the most relevant one to display alongside the memory. Call this after search_facebook_albums when you want to show a photo.

album_id.description: The numeric album ID returned by search_facebook_albums

tools.name: get_album_images

tools.description: Search Facebook posts where the post description partially matches the input.

description.description: Partial text to search for within Facebook post descriptions

tools.name: search_facebook_posts

tools.description: Retrieve the full content of one or more reference documents by their IDs.

document_ids.description: List of reference document IDs to retrieve

tools.name: get_reference_document

tools.description: Get the title, description, id and tags of all reference documents that are available for task.
tools.name: get_available_reference_documents

tools.description: List all relationship complete profiles stored for this archive: each entry is a contact name plus whether generation is still in progress. Use get_complete_profile to read the full text for a name.
tools.name: list_complete_profiles

tools.description: Retrieve the generated relationship/psychological profile text for a specific person by exact name (as shown in list_complete_profiles). If pending is true, the profile is not ready yet.

name.description: Contact name matching a complete profile row

tools.name: get_complete_profile
